title: Penny Arcade
alt: Of course, Penny Arcade has already mocked themselves for this.  They don't care.
transcript:
[A color drawing of Tycho, a man with wild brown hair in blue and cyan colored shirt. He has a big open mouth and holds one arm up while the other may be in his (unseen) pocket. He has two speech bubbles,]
Tycho: You know what? If you've never played the 1995 SNES RPG "Seiken Densetsu", don't even bother reading today's strip.
Tycho: We don't need your kind here.
explanation:
Penny Arcade is a popular web comic that focuses on video game culture. The character in the comic is Tycho Brahe, one of the two main characters of the webcomic (the other being Jonathan "Gabe" Gabriel). Penny Arcade has a reputation for making obscure references to video games without explaining, expecting the reader to be as well-versed in gaming culture as they are, represented by the attitude shown in this comic. 'Seiken Densetsu', as mentioned in the strip, probably refers to Seiken Densetsu 3, an  (Action-RPG) released for the  (SNES) in Japan in 1995, later to be known by the English title of . The game was neither released in the North American region nor officially translated to English until the release of  on June 11, 2019, more than thirteen years after this strip. However, many North American game players might also have recognized Seiken Densetsu 2, the predecessor in the series, by its North American name of .

The title text acknowledges they know they behave like this, but they are indifferent to it and even joke about it into their own comics.
